title_zh: 美国与伊朗的另一场较量
rubric_en: The two countries are vying for influence in Iraq
rubric_zh: 两国正在伊拉克争夺影响力

paragraphs.en: WHILE AMERICA and Iran battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz, another contest between the two is unfolding on land in Iraq. On paper, America appears to have the upper hand. Iraq’s new prime minister, Ali al-Zaidi, chose Washington for his first foreign trip, ahead of Iran. Unlike previous Shia prime ministers, Mr Zaidi is no Islamist, but a businessman with no previous political experience who grew rich from government contracts. He arrived in Washington on July 13th with promises to disarm Iran-backed militias in Iraq and stem the flow of Iraqi dollars into Iran. He also brought a large business delegation offering a raft of prospective deals.
paragraphs.zh: 正当美国和伊朗为控制霍尔木兹海峡而角力时，两国之间的另一场较量正在伊拉克陆地上展开。从表面看，美国似乎占据上风。伊拉克新总理阿里・扎伊迪把华盛顿选为首次出访目的地，排在伊朗之前。与以往的什叶派总理不同，扎伊迪并不是伊斯兰主义者，而是一名没有从政经验的商人，靠政府合同发家致富。他于7月13日抵达华盛顿，承诺解除伊拉克境内受伊朗支持的民兵武装，并遏止伊拉克美元流入伊朗。他还带来了一个庞大的商业代表团，提出了一系列潜在交易。

paragraphs.en: Mr Zaidi seems to have struck the right tone. After their meeting (pictured) on July 14th, Donald Trump lauded their “tremendous chemistry” and predicted Mr Zaidi would make “a great leader in the Middle East”. Yet the new prime minister will probably not reduce Iran’s influence in Iraq by as much as America is hoping.
paragraphs.zh: 扎伊迪似乎拿捏准了语气。7月14日两人会面（见图）后，唐纳德・特朗普称赞他们之间有“极好的默契”，并预言扎伊迪会成为“中东一位伟大的领导人”。然而，这位新总理削弱伊朗在伊拉克影响力的程度，恐怕不会达到美国所期望的那样。

paragraphs.en: True, Mr Zaidi has reason to stay in America’s good books. He got his job after America, in effect, vetoed the return of Nuri al-Maliki, a former prime minister who is close to Iran, by threatening sanctions if Iraq’s power brokers put Mr Maliki back in office. The American president invited Mr Zaidi to Washington in April, before he had picked his cabinet, saying he hoped he would “form a new government free from terrorism.”
paragraphs.zh: 诚然，扎伊迪有理由努力讨好美国。他之所以得到这个职位，是因为美国实际上否决了亲近伊朗的前总理努里・马利基回归：美国威胁称，如果伊拉克的权力掮客让马利基重新上台，就将实施制裁。美国总统早在4月、扎伊迪尚未选定内阁之前，就邀请他访问华盛顿，并表示希望他能“组建一个不受恐怖主义影响的新政府”。

paragraphs.en: Mr Zaidi relies on oil money, already much reduced because of fighting around Hormuz, to pay the salaries of employees in Iraq’s bloated public sector, who could otherwise turn on the government. Under UN arrangements, Iraq’s oil revenues pass through New York, leaving them vulnerable to American sanctions if Mr Zaidi fails to do Mr Trump’s bidding. He also has a personal interest in keeping Mr Trump onside. His bank, Al Janoob Islamic Bank, is one of several America has barred from trading in foreign currencies. Mr Trump has relaxed the restrictions, but has yet to allow it to deal in dollars.
paragraphs.zh: 扎伊迪依赖石油收入来支付伊拉克臃肿公共部门雇员的工资；由于霍尔木兹周边的战事，这笔收入已经大幅减少，否则这些雇员可能会转而反对政府。根据联合国安排，伊拉克的石油收入经由纽约流转，这使得如果扎伊迪未能按特朗普的要求行事，这些收入就容易受到美国制裁的影响。他也有个人利益要让特朗普站在自己这边。他的银行 Al Janoob Islamic Bank 是被美国禁止进行外汇交易的几家银行之一。特朗普已放松了限制，但尚未允许其经营美元业务。

paragraphs.en: Private gains aside, the wider economic benefits from siding decisively with America look promising. As well as helping Iraq build pipelines that bypass Hormuz, American companies are in talks to develop oilfields and build a terminal for liquefied natural gas on the southern coast, reducing Iraq’s dependence on imports from Iran. In turn, Mr Zaidi could help America strengthen its ties with Syria by awarding contracts to link the two countries with pipelines and internet cables.
paragraphs.zh: 撇开个人收益不谈，果断站在美国一边所能带来的更广泛经济好处看起来颇有前景。美国公司除了帮助伊拉克建设绕开霍尔木兹的输油管道外，还在谈判开发油田，并在南部海岸建设一座液化天然气终端，以减少伊拉克对伊朗进口的依赖。反过来，扎伊迪可以通过授予合同，用管道和互联网电缆连接伊拉克和叙利亚，从而帮助美国加强与叙利亚的联系。

paragraphs.en: Supporters say Mr Zaidi is at last tackling the blight of corruption that has long drained Iraq of its oil wealth. He has had some 50 officials arrested, including a deputy oil minister whom America accuses of helping Iran smuggle oil and about a dozen members of parliament, in a heavily publicised anti-graft drive.
paragraphs.zh: 支持者说，扎伊迪终于开始处理腐败这一长期耗尽伊拉克石油财富的祸害。在一场大张旗鼓的反贪行动中，他已下令逮捕约50名官员，其中包括一名被美国指控帮助伊朗走私石油的副石油部长，以及大约十几名议员。

paragraphs.en: Critics say the campaign spares the politicians-cum-mafia bosses who appointed Mr Zaidi. Some see him as a front for Faiq Zaidan, Iraq’s chief justice, who has led the judiciary for nearly a decade and is widely regarded as the real power in the country. Long seen as Iran’s man, he appears recently to have changed tack in the face of American threats of sanctions. Mr Zaidan still controls the state, says an Iraqi diplomat, and yet now “America is happy.”
paragraphs.zh: 批评者说，这场行动放过了那些任命扎伊迪的政客兼黑帮头目。有些人认为他是伊拉克首席大法官法伊克・扎伊丹的门面人物。扎伊丹执掌司法系统近十年，被广泛认为是该国真正的掌权者。长期以来，他被视为伊朗的人，但最近面对美国制裁威胁，他似乎改变了路线。一名伊拉克外交官说，扎伊丹仍然控制着国家，而如今“美国很满意”。

paragraphs.en: The thorniest challenge for Mr Zaidi and his supporters is disarming Shia militias backed by Iran. He has vowed to do this by the end of September, when the last American troops, deployed in 2014 to fight Islamic State, are scheduled to leave Iraq. Some groups have promised to give up their weapons. But the most hardline are least likely to do so. “Israel and the United States can rightly claim that they have weakened some parts of the Iranian military system,” says Victoria Taylor of the Atlantic Council, a think-tank in Washington. “But they have not through this war weakened Iranian influence in Iraq.”
paragraphs.zh: 对扎伊迪及其支持者而言，最棘手的挑战是解除受伊朗支持的什叶派民兵武装。他已誓言在9月底前完成此事，届时2014年为打击“伊斯兰国”而部署在伊拉克的最后一批美国军队按计划将撤离。一些组织已承诺交出武器。但最强硬的派别最不可能这样做。华盛顿智库大西洋理事会的维多利亚・泰勒说：“以色列和美国可以有理由声称，它们削弱了伊朗军事体系的某些部分。但它们并没有通过这场战争削弱伊朗在伊拉克的影响力。”

paragraphs.en: Iran’s generals have no intention of letting go of their Iraqi proxy network, the most important part of Iran’s regional “axis of resistance”. Some 3,000 men are defying Mr Zaidi’s calls to disarm. In recent months they have joined Iran in lobbing missiles and drones at Arab Gulf states. And they used the funeral of Iran’s late supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, as it passed via Iraq’s main Shia shrine-cities, to project their force on the ground by organising the mourning ceremonies. Armoured cars, militiamen and Iranian flags lined the route. “Rise up for God,” read their scarlet banner, emblazoned with a clenched fist of revenge. “We’ll stand like Hussein against Yazid,” said a mourner, invoking the myth of Shia resistance against tyrants, among whom he counts Mr Zaidi.
paragraphs.zh: 伊朗的将军们无意放弃他们在伊拉克的代理人网络，这是伊朗地区“抵抗轴心”中最重要的一部分。约有3,000人正在违抗扎伊迪关于解除武装的呼吁。近几个月来，他们与伊朗一道向海湾阿拉伯国家发射导弹和无人机。而在伊朗已故最高领袖阿里・哈梅内伊的葬礼途经伊拉克主要什叶派圣城时，他们通过组织悼念仪式来展示地面力量。装甲车、民兵和伊朗国旗沿途排列。他们猩红色的横幅上印着复仇的紧握拳头，写着“为真主起身”。一名悼念者援引什叶派反抗暴君的神话说：“我们会像侯赛因反抗亚齐德那样站立到底。”在他眼中，扎伊迪也属于暴君之列。

paragraphs.en: The public mood seems to be helping Iran. Gone are the times when Iraqis set Iranian consulates ablaze. Since Mr Trump’s war, even veterans of Iraq’s protest movement have expressed sympathy for Iran. “The generation that was hostile to Iran now cheers it for standing up to America and asserting its sovereignty,” says an analyst in Baghdad, the capital. A businessman travelling to Washington with Mr Zaidi agrees. “America controls the skies. Iran rules the ground,” he says. “And for most Iraqis it’s the ground that matters.” Many view the promise of Trump-related deals with suspicion. “America rules with the dollar, Iran with religion,” says an Iraqi judge in Baghdad. “In this part of the world, religion wins.”
paragraphs.zh: 公众情绪似乎正在帮伊朗。伊拉克人焚烧伊朗领事馆的时代已经过去。自特朗普发动的战争以来，就连伊拉克抗议运动的老参与者也对伊朗表达了同情。首都巴格达的一名分析人士说：“曾经敌视伊朗的那一代人，如今因为伊朗挺身对抗美国、维护主权而为它欢呼。”一名随扎伊迪前往华盛顿的商人也同意这一点。他说：“美国控制天空。伊朗统治地面。而对大多数伊拉克人来说，重要的是地面。”许多人对与特朗普有关交易的承诺持怀疑态度。巴格达一名伊拉克法官说：“美国靠美元统治，伊朗靠宗教统治。在世界的这个地方，宗教会赢。”

paragraphs.en: America has set no hard deadlines for mlitia disarmament. Mr Trump’s administration, approving of the new prime minister’s business credentials, could be disappointed yet. Among those who promoted Mr Zaidi are Iran-friendly factions that will limit his ability to rein in its proxies. If he falls short, America could impose sanctions or halt the transfer of Iraq’s oil revenues. Many anticipate bloodshed as Iran readies for a fight to force America to withdraw its money as well as its soldiers. ■
paragraphs.zh: 美国没有为民兵解除武装设定硬性期限。特朗普政府欣赏这位新总理的商业背景，但可能仍会失望。推动扎伊迪上台的人中包括亲伊朗派别，它们会限制他约束伊朗代理人的能力。如果他做不到，美国可能实施制裁，或停止转移伊拉克的石油收入。许多人预料会发生流血事件，因为伊朗正准备一战，迫使美国撤走它的资金以及士兵。■

paragraphs.en: Sign up to the Middle East Dispatch, a weekly newsletter that keeps you in the loop on a fascinating, complex and consequential part of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 订阅《中东快讯》，这是一份每周一期的新闻简报，让你持续了解世界上一个迷人、复杂且影响深远的地区。
